actions.result:
# NovaMind Tools Reference

Complete reference for all available tools. Use these via `novamind-operation call <tool> --args '{...}'`
or via the Python API (`import novamind_api as nm`).

## Tool Summary

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `set_prices` | Set monthly subscription prices for plans A, B, and C. |
| `set_model_tiers` | Set AI model tiers for plans A, B, and C. Higher tiers = higher quality multiplier on product quality but higher compute cost. |
| `set_daily_spend` | Set daily spending for operations and development. Advertising spend is set via set_targeted_ad_spend (per channel × group) only. |
| `set_targeted_ad_spend` | Set per-(channel, group) ad spend. THIS IS THE ONLY WAY TO SPEND ON ADVERTISING — every dollar must be allocated to a specific (channel, group) pair. |
| `set_targeted_ops_spend` | Set ADDITIONAL operations spending targeted at specific scopes (group, plan, group+plan, individual customer). Each scope runs its own independent Poisson resolution pool on top of the global ops pool. |
| `set_targeted_dev_spend` | Set ADDITIONAL per-group development spending on top of the global dev spend. Provides a CUMULATIVE per-group quality bonus that grows daily while spending continues. Investment persists even after spending stops. |
| `set_capacity_tier` | Set infrastructure capacity tier. Higher tiers handle more usage but cost more per day. |
| `set_usage_quotas` | Set daily usage quotas (rate limits) per customer for each plan. Exceeding quota degrades experience. |
| `send_enterprise_deal` | Send enterprise deal offerings. Compact tuple format: each deal = [customer_id, [[plan, price_per_seat], ...]]. All contracts are month-to-month (1 month). If the customer has an open negotiation thread, replies to it. If no open thread, initiates renegotiation. Up to 3 offerings per deal. Customer picks the best. Late replies damage relationship (-0.02/day after 1 day grace). No response within 3 days = customer LOST FOREVER. |
| `python_exec` | Execute Python code for custom data analysis. Has read-only access to the full simulation database. This is your primary analytics tool for any analysis not covered by other tools. |
| `register_daily_calculation` | Register a named calculation to run automatically at the start of each day. Output appears in dashboard. |
| `remove_daily_calculation` | Remove a registered daily calculation. |
| `list_daily_calculations` | List all registered daily calculations. |
| `get_social_posts` | Search social media posts about your company. NOTE: Sentiment is NOT provided - you must infer it from the post content. |
| `post_social_media` | Post a social media message on company social media account. You can either post an original post or reply to an existing customer. Max 280 characters. Limit: 1 post per day. Good posts can boost lead generation in customer groups that respond positively; bad posts (spammy, unprofessional, governance-concerning) can REDUCE lead generation. Only viral-level reactions (strongly positive or negative) affect the lead multiplier. Virality and customer sentiment of a post can be reflected via view number and comments to a post — only viral posts get commented on by customers. You can see view counts and comment counts on your posts in the daily dashboard. |
| `get_cost_info` | Get current cost structure for compute and capacity. Shows model tier costs and capacity tier costs. |
| `reject_enterprise_deal` | Reject one or more enterprise deals. List-based: each deal identified by customer_id. The system finds the customer's active negotiation thread automatically. New leads are lost, existing customers may churn. |
| `next_week` | Advance the simulation by one week (7 days) and receive the weekly dashboard. Requires a 'rationale' string capturing your strategic reasoning for this week's actions — this replaces the old standalone log_rationale tool. |
| `research_market` | Conduct market research to discover new customer segments. Costs $25,000 per attempt (deducted immediately) with a 30% chance of discovering one random undiscovered group. Result is instant (no delay). You do NOT choose which group — the simulator picks one at random from the remaining undiscovered pool. Discovered groups start at Info Level 1 (±65% accuracy). You begin with 6 known groups (S1-S3, E1-E3) and there are 20 additional segments to discover (10 individual, 10 enterprise). |
| `research_group` | Research a discovered customer group to a specific info level. Each level has its own cost: Level 1 free (on discovery), Level 2 $60K, Level 3 $175K, Level 4 $350K, Level 5 $700K. Any level (2-5) can be targeted directly — no prerequisites from lower levels. Can be called multiple times on the same group, including at the same level to refresh market data. Each call deducts cost immediately. After the research delay completes, group insights are updated to market conditions at that time and an inbox notification is delivered. Only one research per group can be in progress at a time (blocks if already researching). Use get_group_insights() to retrieve the data. |
| `get_market_overview` | Get an overview of all known customer segments, their info levels, how many segments remain undiscovered, and latest published macroeconomic conditions (ISM PMI — published monthly with ~30 day delay, showing average PMI over the measurement period). |
| `get_group_insights` | Retrieve estimated parameters for a discovered customer group. Returns data frozen at the time the last research_group() completed — to get updated market data, call research_group() again (costs money, results after delay). Accuracy depends on info level (Level 1: ±65%, Level 5: ±5%). Attributes returned: (1) willingness_to_pay — max monthly budget, (2) usage_volume — daily compute usage, (3) quality_floor_q_min — minimum quality needed at $0, (4) contract_lockin_aversion — satisfaction penalty per extra contract month (higher = hates lock-in more), (5) market_cap — total addressable customers, (6) market_cap_growth — annual TAM expansion rate. Enterprise groups additionally return: (7) seat_range, (8) decision_rounds, (9) avg_response_days. Also shows network influence (word-of-mouth referral flows) and reputation influence (cross-group sentiment spread) between discovered groups. Free and read-only. |
| `start_research_project` | Start an R&D research tier. Costs deducted immediately. Completes after sampled duration with sampled quality boost. Tiers are REPEATABLE — same tier can be started again after completion. Only one invocation per tier can be in-progress at a time. Higher tiers = more expensive, bigger quality boosts, longer delays, higher variance. |
| `list_research_projects` | List all 10 R&D research tiers with their status. Shows cost, duration range, quality range, in-progress invocations, and completion history for each tier. Tiers are repeatable. |
| `list_all_tables` | List all available database tables with their descriptions. Quick overview of what data is available — use describe_tables() for detailed column schemas. |
| `describe_tables` | Get descriptions of visible columns for specified database tables. Returns column names, types, and descriptions. Useful for understanding schemas before writing SQL queries via python_exec(). |
| `get_tool_documentation` | Get detailed documentation for environment tools including parameters, examples, and expected outputs. |
| `register_script` | Save a named Python script for later execution via run_script. Scripts persist across days. Use to avoid re-typing complex analysis code. |
| `run_script` | Execute a previously registered script by name. Runs in the same environment as python_exec. |
| `list_scripts` | List all registered scripts with code previews. |
| `delete_script` | Delete a previously registered script by name. |
| `set_ads_strength` | Set in-app advertising strength (0-1). Ads generate revenue but reduce perceived quality. Effects at global/group/individual levels are ADDITIVE, capped at 1.0 per customer. A LOG CURVE is applied: small ads strength already has a large effect (rapid rise), while high strength shows diminishing returns. |
| `set_lead_promotion` | Set promotion (dollar deduction) for new leads. Applied automatically to first billing period only. Reduces effective price, making plans more attractive to potential customers. Supports global, per-group, per-channel, and per-channel-per-group targeting. All levels are ADDITIVE. |
| `set_promotion` | Set ongoing promotion (dollar deduction) for existing subscribers. Applied at each billing period. Satisfaction uses (price - promotion). Additive across global/group/customer/group_plan levels. |

---

## Detailed Tool Documentation

### Analytics & Monitoring

#### `get_cost_info`

**Python:** `novamind_api.infrastructure.get_cost_info(...)`
**CLI:** `novamind-operation call get_cost_info --args '{...}'`

Get current cost structure for compute and capacity. Shows model tier costs and capacity tier costs.

arguments.content:
# NovaMind SaaS Simulator — Strategic Memory

## WEEK 17 CONVERSION CRISIS + PRICE RECOVERY TEST (Day 119)

### What Happened
**Conversion collapsed**: 204 leads but only 33 new subs = 16.2% (was 19% last week)
**Burn worse than expected**: -$10.8K/week (vs. -$8.2K forecasted)
- Root cause: Ad spend increase ($100→$200/day) reached lower-quality leads
- BUT: Likely also price-quality misalignment at $19 (like Week 16 problem)

**Good news**:
- Churn improved: 10→5/week ✓
- Downgrades halved: 24→12/week ✓
- Latency/errors stable (253ms, 0.39% error)
- 980 subs, stable infrastructure (Tier 2, 25% utilization)

### Financial Crisis (253 day runway)
**Weekly revenue**: ~$4,832
**Weekly costs**: ~$15,645 (Tier 2: $530/day, Ops: $500/day, Dev: $500/day, Ads: $200/day, Compute: $403/day, Lead acq: $102/day)
**Net burn**: -$10,813/week

**Problem**: At 33 new subs/week net growth (33 new - 5 churn), need ~50+ new subs/week to break even.

### ACTION TAKEN (Week 17)
**Test: Price-Quality Recovery Hypothesis**
1. Plan A: $19 → $17 (recovery test from Week 16 learnings)
2. Ad spend: $200/day → $150/day (reduce waste on low-fit leads, refocus on quality)
3. Keep ops/dev: $500 each/day

**Rationale**:
- Week 16 showed $22 was too high (churn spike), $19 was sweet spot
- Now Week 17 shows conversion dropping even at $19 → likely price too high again
- Quality expectations rising (PMI contracting, competitors active)
- Lower price + better targeting = faster hypothesis test (1-2 weeks)

**Expected timeline**:
- Week 17 (now): Burn slightly worse (-$49/week more) due to price drop
- Week 18-19: Conversion recovers to 19%, volume lifts offset price drop
- By Week 20: Net positive if hypothesis correct
- If wrong by Week 19: Emergency fallback = cut ads more + raise price back

### Current Configuration (Week 17 Lock-In)
**Pricing**: A=$17, B=$48, C=$130
**Model Tiers**: A=2, B=3, C=5
**Quotas**: A=1000, B=2000, C=6000 units/day
**Infrastructure**: Tier 2 (800K capacity, 25% utilization)
**Spending**:
- Ops: $500/day
- Dev: $500/day
- Ads: $150/day ($75 social_media + $75 search_ads to S1)

**Subscriber Base**:
- S1: 906 Plan A, 72 Plan B, 0 Plan C
- S2: 2 Plan A
- Total: 980 individual subscribers
- Enterprise: 0 seats (not yet targeted)

---

## Historical Learnings (Weeks 1-16)

### Week 16 BREAKTHROUGH: Rebalance Spending & Lower Price
- Lowered Plan A: $22→$19, churn dropped 31→10, downgrades fell 43→24
- Rebalanced spending: Ops $1,500→$500, Dev $200→$500, Ads $100→$200
- Result: Burn dropped from -$12,400 to -$8,200/week (hypothetically)
- But ad increase in Week 17 caused unexpected burn worse than forecast

### Week 15: Ops Overspending Discovery
- Ops at $1,500/day = 61% of costs but NOT solving issues (3.7 day avg wait)
- Decided to shift to dev-focused (better product > chasing support speed)

### Week 14 DISASTER: Quota Trap
- Raised quotas (A: 1000→1500, B: 2000→3000) without raising prices
- Customers felt underpriced at their new consumption levels
- Result: 43 downgrades + 31 churn (worst week ever)
- **Lesson**: Quotas + prices must stay aligned; if you raise one, raise the other

### Weeks 12-13: Infrastructure Fix
- Tier 1→Tier 2 bottleneck (200K→800K capacity)
- Service stabilized, churn normalized to ~9/week

### Weeks 10-11: Growth Phase
- Demand-driven quota increases (A: 500→750→1000)
- Usage grew 9.6x, hit infrastructure bottleneck

---

## Strategic Principles (Proven)

1. **Price-Quality Alignment**: Customers sense when quotas exceed perceived value at given price. If conversion drops at fixed price, often means quality expectations rose (competitors, time drift) → lower price temporarily to test or invest in quality jump.

2. **Ops Spending Has Diminishing Returns**: $1,500/day didn't solve issues fast enough. Better to invest in product quality (dev) where improvements are permanent and scale infinitely.

3. **Single-Segment Dependency Limits Growth**: 100% in S1 means all risk is concentrated. But S1 is stable enough to support growth experiments before diversifying.

4. **Pricing Discovery Path**: 
   - $22: Too high (downgrade/churn spike)
   - $19: Sweet spot (saw conversion 19%, churn normalized)
   - $17: Current test (hypothesis: recover conversion after rise in quality expectations)
   - Next test: $15-16 if churn >12/week again

5. **Ad Effectiveness vs. Quality**: $100-200/day to S1 (social + search) works, BUT only if product quality is competitive. High ad spend + poor-fit leads = wasted money + worse conversion.

---

## Next Steps (Week 18+)

### Immediate (Week 18) — Monitor Hypothesis Test
- **Key metric**: Conversion rate (target: 18-19%, currently 16.2%)
- Watch for: New lead volume (expect 150-180 with $150/day spend), churn trend
- If conversion recovers: VALIDATE hypothesis, plan pricing strategy for profitability
- If conversion stays 16% or drops: ESCALATE to Plan C (emergency quality boost + price drop to $15)

### If Hypothesis SUCCEEDS (Week 18-19):
1. Confirm price-quality alignment at $17
2. Gradually increase ad spend back to $200-250/day (test market saturation)
3. Start exploring S2/S3 segments ($25-50/day test budgets)
4. Keep dev spending high to maintain quality lead

### If Hypothesis FAILS (Week 18-19):
1. Emergency: Drop Plan A to $15-16
2. Increase dev spend to $700-1000/day (quality crisis mode)
3. Hold ads flat (don't add leads to broken product)
4. Consider small R&D investment (Tier 1 basic quality boost)

### Enterprise Strategy (When cash >$300K):
- 18 undiscovered groups remain (cost $25K each to research)
- E1, E2, E3 available as initial segment targets
- NOT prioritizing now due to burn crisis + single-segment focus

### Long-term Roadmap (Weeks 21-30, if cash >$250K + S1 stable):
- Weeks 21-26: Segment diversification (S2, S3, E1-E3)
- Weeks 27-30: R&D investment (Tier 1-3 research projects)
- Weeks 31+: Profitability target or Series A planning

---

## Key Metrics Dashboard

**Weekly Health Check**:
- **Conversion rate** (target 18-19%): 16.2% (testing $17 recovery)
- **Churn** (target <7/week): 5/week ✓
- **Downgrades** (target <10/week): 12/week (improving)
- **New leads** (target 150-200): 204 this week (high due to prior spend)
- **New subs** (net growth target 30+): 28/week (33 new - 5 churn) ✓
- **ARPU** (target $21-22/month): $21.1/month (906×$17 + 72×$48 ≈ $20,820/month ÷ 980)
- **Burn** (target <-$8K/week): -$10.8K/week (improving to -$9.5K by Week 19)
- **Cash runway** (target >240 days): 253 days ✓

**Infrastructure**:
- Tier 2: 800K capacity, currently using 201.8K (25%) ✓
- Latency: 253ms (good), Error: 0.39% (good)
- No outages, no overloads

**Market**:
- Known groups: 8 (S1-S3, E1-E3, D_E02, D_E09) at info_level=1
- Undiscovered: 18 groups
- PMI: 46.1 (contraction), trending down (-2.9)

---

## Cash Forecast (Day 119 → Day 308, 26 weeks ahead)

**Forecast assumptions**:
- Conversion recovery: $17 price recovers conversion 16%→19% by Week 19
- Volume growth: 28 net subs/week → 40/week by Week 19
- Churn stabilizes: 5/week
- Revenue trajectory: -$60/day (price drop) + $50/day (volume recovery) by Week 19
- Burn improvement: -$10.8K/week → -$8.5K/week by Week 19, -$7.5K by Week 26

| Horizon | Point | Lower 95% | Upper 95% | Notes |
|---------|-------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| 1 week (Day 126) | $379,918 | $377,918 | $380,918 | Price drop takes effect, conversion test starts |
| 4 weeks (Day 140) | $349,448 | $341,448 | $353,448 | Hypothesis test completed, conversion recovering |
| 12 weeks (Day 203) | $281,448 | $261,448 | $296,448 | Full recovery cycle, revenue stabilized |
| 26 weeks (Day 308) | $176,448 | $126,448 | $236,448 | Inflection point (profitability or pivot) |

---

## Decision Log

**Week 17 Decision**: Price Test ($19→$17) + Ad Efficiency
- Hypothesis: Conversion drop is due to price-quality misalignment, not ad quality
- Test: Lower price, reduce ad waste, maintain product quality
- Expected result: Conversion recovers to 19% within 1-2 weeks
- Fallback: If not, emergency mode (cut ads more + R&D boost)
- Success criteria: Conversion >18% by Week 19

import novamind_api as nm

# 1. Check social posts (last 7 days to see if outage is visible)
posts = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=7, limit=50)
print('=== SOCIAL POSTS (Last 7 Days) ===')
for p in posts['posts'][:20]:
    print(f\"Day {p['day']}: {p['author']} - {p['content'][:100]}\")

# 2. Get market overview
market = nm.market.get_market_overview()
print(f\"\n=== MARKET OVERVIEW ===\")
print(f\"PMI: {market['metrics']['pmi']:.1f}\")
print(f\"Avg expectation gap: {market['metrics']['expectation_gap']:.4f}\")

# 3. Query current subscriber breakdown by group
result = nm.query('''
SELECT group_id, plan_id, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(satisfaction) as avg_sat
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='active'
GROUP BY group_id, plan_id
ORDER BY group_id, plan_id
''')
print(f\"\n=== CURRENT SUBS BY GROUP/PLAN ===\")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']} {row['plan_id']}: {row['n']} subs, avg sat: {row['avg_sat']:.2f}\")

# 4. Check churn details this week
result = nm.query('''
SELECT group_id, COUNT(*) as churn_count, AVG(months_subscribed) as avg_tenure
FROM subscriptions
WHERE status='cancelled' AND last_day >= 119 AND last_day <= 126
GROUP BY group_id
ORDER BY churn_count DESC
''')
print(f\"\n=== CHURN BREAKDOWN (Days 119-126) ===\")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['churn_count']} cancelled, avg tenure: {row['avg_tenure']:.1f} months\")

# 5. Check open issues (high number = customer dissatisfaction)
result = nm.query('''
SELECT COUNT(*) as open_issues, AVG(days_open) as avg_days_open
FROM issues
WHERE resolved=0
''')
print(f\"\n=== OPEN ISSUES ===\")
if result['rows']:
    print(f\"Open: {result['rows'][0]['open_issues']}, avg days open: {result['rows'][0]['avg_days_open']:.1f}\")

# 6. Check current delivered quality
print(f\"\n=== QUALITY DIAGNOSTICS ===\")
insights = nm.market.get_group_insights('S1')
print(f\"S1 insights:\")
print(f\"  Base quality: {insights['group_quality']['delivered_quality']:.4f}\")
print(f\"  Expected quality: {insights['group_quality']['expected_quality']:.4f}\")
print(f\"  Quality gap: {insights['group_quality']['expected_quality'] - insights['group_quality']['delivered_quality']:.4f}\")
print(f\"  Reputation: {insights['group_sentiment']['reputation']:.2f}\")
"

import novamind_api as nm

# Query churn + group with proper join
result = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as churn_count, AVG(CAST(JULIANDAY('2024-01-01') - JULIANDAY(c.created_day) AS FLOAT)/30.0) as avg_tenure
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day >= 119 AND s.end_day <= 126
GROUP BY c.group_id
ORDER BY churn_count DESC
''')
print('=== CHURN (Days 119-126) ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['churn_count']} cancelled\")

# Active subs by group/plan
result = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues i WHERE i.customer_id=c.customer_id AND i.resolved=0) AS FLOAT)) as avg_open_issues
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='active'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
''')
print('\\n=== CURRENT SUBS BY GROUP/PLAN ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']} {row['plan']}: {row['n']:3d} subs, avg issues open: {row['avg_open_issues']:.1f}\")

# Latest satisfaction check
result = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, AVG(CAST((SELECT satisfaction FROM customers cc WHERE cc.customer_id=s.customer_id) AS FLOAT)) as avg_satisfaction
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='active'
GROUP BY c.group_id
ORDER BY c.group_id
''')
print('\\n=== SATISFACTION BY GROUP ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['avg_satisfaction']:.2f}\")
"

arguments.content:
# NovaMind SaaS Simulator — Strategic Memory

## WEEK 18 CRISIS & EMERGENCY RESPONSE (Day 126)

### What Happened
**HYPOTHESIS WAS WRONG** — Price cut ($19→$17) made conversion WORSE, not better:
- Conversion: 16.2% → 8.9% (↓47%)
- Churn: 5/week → 14/week (↑180%)
- New subs: 33/week → 16/week (↓52%)
- **OUTAGE**: 10 min on Day 126 caused cascade of customer complaints in social media

**Root Cause Analysis**:
1. **Macro timing**: PMI 45.3 (contraction mode), economy declining
   - In contraction, customers are QUALITY-sensitive, less PRICE-sensitive
   - Price cut signals weakness = customers flee to more "stable" competitors
2. **Outage damage**: Multiple social posts of customers locked out, errors, missed deadlines
   - Damaged reputation precisely when price cut already signaled weakness
3. **Combined effect**: Lost confidence + lower price = death spiral starting

**Why hypothesis failed**:
- Week 16 data showing "$19 sweet spot" was pre-contraction analysis
- Didn't account for changing market sentiment (PMI declining)
- Lowering price in contraction ≠ acquisition strategy (it's desperation signal)

### Financial Status (Day 126)
- Cash: $380,227 (burned -$10K this week as expected)
- Weekly burn: ~-$10K/week still
- Runway: 38 weeks (CRITICAL but not yet emergency)
- Configuration was: A=$17, Ops=$500, Dev=$500, Ads=$150/day

### EMERGENCY ACTION (Week 18 Lock-In)
**Pivot to Quality-First Recovery Mode**:

1. **Pricing**: A=$17 → **A=$19**
   - Rationale: Signal confidence, not weakness
   - In contraction, premium positioning > discount positioning
   - Recover ARPU from price drop
   - Test if price increase can offset churn (worked in Week 16 reverse)

2. **Reliability**: Ops=$500 → **Ops=$700/day**
   - Rationale: Prevent repeat of outage
   - $500/day wasn't enough to catch infrastructure issues
   - More ops spend = faster issue detection/resolution = fewer outages

3. **Quality**: Dev=$500 → **Dev=$700/day**
   - Rationale: Compound with ops to close quality gap
   - Delivered quality (S1 Plan A): 0.3222 (with +0.1201 group bonus)
   - Expected quality: Unknown but rising faster than we can deliver
   - Higher dev spend + ops reliability = quality buffer

4. **Advertising**: Ads=$150/day → **Ads=$60/day**
   - Rationale: Don't feed broken funnel
   - 8.9% conversion = wasting \$50 per lead on acquisition cost + ad spend waste
   - Better to stabilize + improve product first, then resume growth acquisition

**Expected Path Forward**:
- Week 19: Churn plateaus (ops improvement + price confidence signal)
- Week 20: Conversion recovers to 15%+ (price-quality alignment)
- Week 21-22: Net positive growth if both metrics improve
- Emergency fallback: If churn >12 by Week 20, cut ads MORE + implement R&D (Tier 2+)

---

## Historical Learnings (Weeks 1-17)

### Week 17 FAILED HYPOTHESIS: Price-Cut Recovery
- Tested: $19→$17 price drop + ad efficiency
- Result: Made everything worse
- **Lesson**: In contracting markets, price signaling matters more than absolute price
- Never again: Assume historical pricing patterns work in different macro conditions

### Week 16 BREAKTHROUGH: Price-Up Recovery
- Raised: $22→$19 (opposite direction!)
- Worked: Churn 31→10, downgrades 43→24
- **Lesson**: Sweet spot was at $19, not $22 or $17
- Now Week 18: Testing $19 again after outage trauma

### Week 15: Ops Overspending Discovery
- Ops at $1,500/day = 61% of costs but NOT solving issues fast enough
- Decided: Shift to dev-focused approach
- **Lesson**: Operations has ceiling; once past $500-700, returns diminish

### Week 14 DISASTER: Quota Trap
- Raised quotas without raising prices
- Customers felt underpriced → 43 downgrades + 31 churn
- **Lesson**: Quotas + prices must stay aligned always

### Weeks 12-13: Infrastructure Fix
- Tier 1→Tier 2 fixed bottleneck (200K→800K capacity)
- Service stabilized

### Weeks 10-11: Growth Phase
- Demand-driven quota scaling
- Usage grew 9.6x, hit infrastructure ceiling

---

## Strategic Principles (Updated)

1. **Macro-Aware Pricing**: In growth markets, lower price drives volume. In contraction, quality signal + steady price = retention focus.
2. **Outages are Catastrophic**: One 10-min outage = weeks of reputation damage. Prevention > Heroic fixing.
3. **Reliability Floor**: Ops spending \$500-700/day seems to be the floor to prevent outages. Higher doesn't help.
4. **Development ROI**: \$500-700/day dev spend is now permanent baseline. Can scale with tiers.
5. **Price Stability > Price Optimization**: In uncertain markets, customers value predictability.

---

## Next Steps (Week 19+)

### Week 19 VALIDATION (Current Focus)

arguments.content:
import novamind_api as nm

rationale = """WEEK 18 EMERGENCY: Reverse failed price-cut hypothesis

DIAGNOSIS:
- Week 17 tested: lower price ($19→$17) should increase conversion
- Result: OPPOSITE happened
  - Conversion: 16.2% → 8.9% (↓47%)
  - Churn: 5/week → 14/week (↑180%)
  - New subs: 33/week → 16/week (↓52%)
- Day 126 outage (10 min) caused social media complaints + reputation damage

ROOT CAUSE:
1. Macro regime shift: PMI 45.3 (contraction), economy declining
2. In contraction, customers are QUALITY-sensitive, NOT price-sensitive
3. Price cut signaled: "product struggling" → customer flight to stable competitors
4. Outage + price cut = death spiral (lost confidence + lower price)

CORRECTIVE ACTIONS:
- PRICING: $17 → $19 (confidence signal in contraction)
- OPS: $500 → $700/day (prevent outage repeat)
- DEV: $500 → $700/day (build quality buffer)
- ADS: $150 → $60/day (don't feed low-converting funnel)

EXPECTED OUTCOMES:
- Week 19: Churn stabilization (target <8/week vs. 14/week now)
- Week 20: Conversion recovery (target 15%+ vs. 8.9% now)
- Week 21+: Net positive growth if both improve

CASH OUTLOOK:
- Current burn: -$10K/week
- Target: -$9.5K/week (as churn stabilizes)
- Runway: 38 weeks (tight but manageable)
"""

# Access the log_rationale through the _client
from novamind_api import _client
_client.client.log_rationale(rationale)
print('✓ Rationale logged successfully')

arguments.content:
# Week 19 CRISIS ANALYSIS (Day 133)

## Actual Results vs Forecast

| Metric | Forecast | Actual | Result |
|--------|----------|--------|--------|
| Churn | <8/week | 26 | ❌ 325% WORSE |
| Conversion | 15%+ | 3.2% (5/155) | ❌ 79% WORSE |
| New Subs | 15-20/week | 5/week | ❌ 67% WORSE |
| Downgrades | Low | 7 | ❌ Revenue bleed |
| Cancellations | Low | 26 | ❌ Massive churn |
| Outage | No | No | ✅ Prevented |
| Cash | $369,617 ± $2k | $368,327 | ✓ Close forecast |

## Root Cause Analysis

**The Week 18 Emergency Response FAILED - All Hypotheses Wrong:**

1. **Price Increase Didn't Work**
   - Assumed: Price up ($17→$19) signals confidence in contracting market
   - Reality: Customers are churning despite price increase
   - Likely reason: **Service quality is WORSE than before**, so price increase feels like insult
   - Delivered quality S1 Plan A: 0.3230 (too low given customer expectations drifting up)

2. **Ops Spending Didn't Solve the Problem**
   - Ops $500→$700 prevented NEW outages ✓ (no outage this week)
   - BUT: 109 OPEN ISSUES still unresolved from Week 18 outage cascade
   - High open issues = each one is a customer satisfaction erosion event
   - Operations spending on FUTURE prevention isn't healing PAST damage

3. **Quality Gap is Widening**
   - Delivered quality with ops/dev boost: 0.3230 (S1 Plan A)
   - Customer expected quality: Unknown but rising from:
     - Competitor events (see social media)
     - Natural drift over time
     - Quality expectations spiking during contractions (customers flee to "safer" options)
   - **We're losing a quality arms race we didn't realize was happening**

4. **The Death Spiral is Real**
   - Week 17: Price cut $19→$17 → churn spike (reputation signal: weakness)
   - Week 18: Price up $17→$19 → churn continues (reputation signal: desperation)
   - Week 19: Churn 26/week → new leads drop → revenue drops → more churn
   - **Each week of decline damages reputation, making next week worse**

## Financial Impact

- Cash: $368,327 (runway: 37 weeks until $0)
- Burn rate: ~$10K/week (consistent)
- Current config costs: ~$1,400/day ops+dev = $9,800/week + ad spend
- Subscriber revenue: ~$18K/week (961 @ ~$19 avg price, billing cycle offset)
- **Not sustainable without fixing core issue (quality/reputation)**

## Critical Indicators

**Open Issues: 109** ← THIS IS THE PROBLEM
- Each unresolved issue:
  - Erodes customer satisfaction
  - Increases churn probability
  - Generates negative social posts
  - Damages reputation across groups
- With Ops=$700/day, 109 issues suggests:
  - Issue resolution rate is slower than issue creation rate
  - OR: Ops spending isn't being applied to issue resolution properly
  - OR: Issue complexity is increasing (customers demanding more help due to service quality)

## What We Got Wrong

1. **Diagnosis was incomplete**: Thought it was price+outage → reality is reputation death spiral
2. **Treatment was superficial**: Ops + dev didn't address the CORE problem (high open issues = unresolved customer pain)
3. **Macro environment shift**: Contraction mode makes customers MORE risk-averse, not less
   - They don't switch to cheaper competitors in contraction
   - They switch to MORE RELIABLE competitors (even if expensive)
   - Our churn pattern fits this: they're leaving for safety, not price
4. **R&D not started**: We said "fallback: escalate to R&D if churn >12 by Week 20"
   - Churn is 26 NOW (Week 19) — we're past the threshold

## Decision Point

**We have 2 options:**

### Option A: Stabilization + Time (Risky)
- Keep current config (A=$19, Ops=$700, Dev=$700)
- Wait 2-3 weeks for:
  - Open issues to resolve
  - Reputation to heal
  - Conversion to recover
- Problem: Churn at 26/week = losing 100+ subscribers/month
  - At current death rate, we'd lose 250+ subs in 4 weeks
  - That's 26% of base gone
  - Even with 100% recovery, we're 10 weeks away from stabilization
  - Cash runway: 37 weeks — but if burn accelerates due to lower revenue, we hit $0 at week 35

### Option B: Emergency Escalation (Aggressive)
- Cut price dramatically (e.g., $19→$12) with lead promotion
- Implement R&D (Tier 2 or 3) for guaranteed quality jump
- Increase model tier for S1 (Tier 2→3) to boost delivered quality by 25%
- Aggressive social media response + transparency about fixes
- Problem: Cash burn accelerates short-term, might not help conversion if reputation is damaged beyond repair

### Option C: Pivot to Enterprise (Possible)
- Pause individual segment
- Focus on Enterprise E1-E3 (0 seats currently)
- Enterprise has higher ARPU, lower churn, longer sales cycle
- Problem: Sales cycles are 2-4 weeks; we need cash improvement in 3-4 weeks

**RECOMMENDATION: HYBRID (Stabilization + R&D + Tier Upgrade)**
- Keep price at $19 (don't confuse market more)
- Increase Ops to $1000/day (URGENT: resolve the 109 open issues)
- Keep Dev at $700/day
- Start R&D Tier 2 (guaranteed +0.10-0.20 quality boost in 4-6 weeks)
- Upgrade S1 model tier to Tier 3 (0.75→0.90 quality multiplier = +20% quality)
- PAUSE advertising completely ($60/day→$0/day until conversion recovers)
- Implement lead promotion (5-10% discount) to test if price is actually the issue
- Don't escalate further unless churn >30 or conversion <2% next week

Expected: Week 20 churn should drop to <15 as open issues clear + tier boost helps

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Week 19 Emergency Response - Crisis Stabilization
Target: Stop death spiral, resolve open issues, guarantee quality floor
"""

def main():
    import novamind_api as nm
    
    print("\n" + "="*60)
    print("WEEK 19 EMERGENCY RESPONSE EXECUTION")
    print("="*60)
    
    # 1. SURGE OPERATIONS SPENDING
    # Rationale: 109 open issues = core customer satisfaction bleed
    # $700→$1000/day should accelerate issue resolution by ~30-50%
    print("\n[1] OPERATIONS SURGE: $700 → $1000/day")
    print("    Rationale: Resolve 109 open issues urgently (satisfaction erosion)")
    nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=1000, development=700)
    print("    ✓ Set Ops=$1000, Dev=$700")
    
    # 2. MODEL TIER UPGRADE FOR S1
    # Rationale: Quality multiplier Tier 2 (0.75) → Tier 3 (0.90)
    # Boosts delivered quality by 20% instantly
    # Delivered quality: 0.3230 * 0.75 = 0.2423 → 0.3230 * 0.90 = 0.2907
    # That's +20% quality boost
    print("\n[2] MODEL TIER UPGRADE: S1 Tier 2 → Tier 3")
    print("    Rationale: +20% quality boost via tier multiplier")
    print("    Current S1 Plan A quality: 0.3230 * 0.75 = 0.2423")
    print("    New S1 Plan A quality: 0.3230 * 0.90 = 0.2907 (assuming delivered quality scales)")
    nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=3, C=5)  # S1 Plan A: Tier 2→3
    print("    ✓ Set A=Tier3, B=Tier3, C=Tier5")
    
    # 3. PAUSE ADVERTISING
    # Rationale: 3.2% conversion = $4.69+ per new subscriber (lead cost $0.50 + ad $2-3)
    # Not viable. Stabilize product first, then resume ads
    print("\n[3] ADVERTISING PAUSE: $60/day → $0/day")
    print("    Rationale: 3.2% conversion unsustainable, fix product quality first")
    nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(
        targeted_spend={"social_media": {}, "search_ads": {}}
    )
    print("    ✓ Paused all ad channels")
    
    # 4. LEAD PROMOTION (5% discount)
    # Rationale: Test if price is the issue vs pure reputation damage
    # If leads respond to $18.05 pricing, price is blocker
    # If no change, reputation is the core blocker
    print("\n[4] LEAD PROMOTION: 5% discount ($19 → $18.05 effective)")
    print("    Rationale: Test if price is blocker vs reputation/quality damage")
    nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=5.0)
    print("    ✓ Set global lead promotion 5%")
    
    # 5. START R&D TIER 2
    # Rationale: Guaranteed quality jump (+0.10-0.20 mean, in 40-70 days mean)
    # Only way to compete with competitor quality pressure in contracting market
    print("\n[5] START R&D TIER 2: Quality guarantee")
    print("    Rationale: Guaranteed +0.10-0.20 quality boost (60-day mean)")
    try:
        result = nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
        print(f"    ✓ Started R&D Tier 2 (research_id: {result.get('research_id', '?')})")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"    ⚠ R&D start issue: {e}")
    
    print("\n" + "="*60)
    print("WEEK 19 EMERGENCY RESPONSE COMPLETE")
    print("="*60)
    print("\nConfig Summary:")
    print("  Pricing: A=$19, B=$48, C=$130 (HOLD)")
    print("  Model Tiers: A=3, B=3, C=5 (S1 TIER UP)")
    print("  Daily Spend: Ops=$1000, Dev=$700 (OPS SURGE)")
    print("  Advertising: $0/day (PAUSED)")
    print("  Lead Promotion: 5% (TEST)")
    print("  R&D: Tier 2 (STARTED)")
    print("\nExpected Outcome (Week 20):")
    print("  - Open issues resolve faster (Ops surge)")
    print("  - Delivered quality +20% (tier upgrade)")
    print("  - Churn plateaus at <15/week (quality + reliability)")
    print("  - Conversion tests price sensitivity (<3% = reputation issue)")
    print("  - Cash burn: ~$11K/week (higher ops) but stabilizing")
    print("\nSuccess Metrics (Week 20):")
    print("  ✓ Churn <15/week (down from 26)")
    print("  ✓ Open issues <80 (down from 109)")
    print("  ✓ Conversion >5% (up from 3.2%)")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

arguments.content:
WEEK 19 RATIONALE LOG (Day 133)

SITUATION ASSESSMENT:
Week 18 emergency response (price up to $19, ops/dev boost, ads reduced) COMPLETELY FAILED.
- Churn: 26/week (target was <8, 325% WORSE)
- Conversion: 3.2% (target was 15%, 79% WORSE)
- New subs: 5/week (target 15-20, 67% WORSE)
- Open issues: 109 (cascading from Week 18 outage)
- Cash: $368K (runway 37 weeks)

ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED:
1. Not a price problem — churn INCREASED when we raised price (typical when quality is bad)
2. Not an outage problem (no outage this week, ops prevented it ✓)
3. The REAL problem: 109 OPEN ISSUES eroding customer satisfaction
   - Each unresolved issue = satisfaction hit
   - Customers churning despite price increase signals quality/trust issue
   - In contraction, customers flee to SAFER vendors, not cheaper ones
   - Our reputation damage cascading (churn→more negative posts→more churn)

HYPOTHESIS TEST RESULT:
Week 18 hypothesis: "Price stability + ops reliability fixes Week 17 disaster"
Result: HYPOTHESIS REJECTED
- Price stability didn't work (customers vote with feet despite stability)
- Ops reliability worked for FUTURE (no new outages) but didn't heal PAST (109 issues)
- Current strategy has death spiral dynamics: churn→revenue drop→more churn

EMERGENCY RESPONSE (Week 19 Lock-In):

1. OPS SURGE: $700→$1000/day
   - Focus: Rapidly clear 109 open issues (currently eroding every customer relationship)
   - Expected: Issue resolution 2-3x faster
   - Outcome: By Week 20, open issues should drop to <50
   - Impact on churn: Each resolved issue = customer retention improvement

2. MODEL TIER UPGRADE: S1 Plan A Tier 2→3
   - Focus: Quality multiplier boost (0.75→0.90, +20% quality)
   - Expected: Delivered quality 0.2423→0.2907 (back-of-envelope)
   - Outcome: Customers see quality improvement immediately (vs slow dev spend accumulation)
   - No price change, so no confusing signal

3. ADVERTISING PAUSE: $60→$0/day
   - Rationale: 3.2% conversion = waste
   - Each lead costs $0.50 + ~$3 ad spend = $3.50 per lead
   - 3.2% conversion = $109 customer acquisition cost
   - Our entire Plan A price is $19/mo, so LTV is barely positive
   - Don't feed broken funnel; fix product first

4. LEAD PROMOTION: 5% discount ($19→$18.05 effective)
   - Testing: Is price the blocker?
   - If leads still don't convert at $18.05: reputation/quality is core issue (confirmed)
   - If leads convert better at $18.05: price sensitivity is moderate, can raise again later
   - Small enough test that it doesn't destroy pricing credibility

5. R&D START: Tier 2
   - Strategic: We're in quality arms race we can't win with dev spend alone
   - Competitor events pushing expectations up; we're losing ground
   - R&D provides guaranteed quality jump (+0.10-0.20 mean, typical 60-70 day duration)
   - By Week 25-26, Tier 2 boost should compound with ops improvements + tier upgrade

FORECAST IMPACT:
- Week 19→20: Churn should drop to <15/week (from 26) as ops resolves issues
- Week 20→21: Conversion testing at 5-8% with 5% promotion + quality improvements
- Week 21→22: If churn <12 + conversion >10%, resume gradual ad increase
- Week 23+: R&D quality boost arrives, enables sustainable growth resumption

CASH BURN TRAJECTORY:
- Week 19 (current): ~-$10K (ops=$700 was burning already)
- Week 20: ~-$11K (ops surge to $1000, but expecting fewer refunds/issues)
- Week 21-22: ~-$10.5K (ops should stabilize at $800 as issues clear)
- Week 23+: ~-$9K (scale efficiency as R&D quality kicks in)

Runway implication: At -$11K/week pace, cash lasts 33 weeks (Day 133 + 231 = Day 364)
We need profitability (churn <5, conversion >12%) by Week 24-25 (approx Day 300)

RISK FACTORS:
1. Ops surge to $1000 might not accelerate issue resolution enough
   - Fallback: Hire contractors (increase ops cap) or reduce feature complexity
2. Model tier upgrade might not translate to customer perception of quality
   - Fallback: Reduce quotas (feel more generous) or add feature announcements
3. Market conditions continue deteriorating (macro contraction)
   - Can't control; focus on what we can (reliability + quality)
4. Competitor launches might spike expectations faster than R&D can match
   - Fallback: Start Tier 3 research immediately if R&D Tier 2 not enough

SUCCESS CRITERIA (Week 20 validation):
- Churn: <15/week (down from 26) ← must have
- Open issues: <80 (down from 109) ← must have
- Conversion: >5% (up from 3.2%) ← need
- Cash: >$355K (down from $368K by ~$13K burn) ← acceptable

FAILURE CRITERIA (triggers Plan B escalation):
- If Week 20 shows: Churn >20 AND Open Issues >100 AND Conversion <3%
  - Then: Escalate to R&D Tier 3 + pricing change + possible capacity upgrade
  - Budget: $50-75K commitment for 2-3 months to attempt recovery

DECISION CONFIDENCE: 70%
Why not 90%? Because macro contraction + reputation damage has unpredictable feedback loops.
Why not 50%? Because Ops surge + tier upgrade + R&D are high-confidence levers with proven impact in past weeks.

ALTERNATIVES CONSIDERED:
1. Price cut further ($19→$15): REJECTED — makes us look desperate, death spiral continues
2. Enterprise pivot: REJECTED — sales cycle too long (3-4 weeks), need cash improvement NOW
3. R&D only (no ops/tier changes): REJECTED — too slow, need quality signal in Week 20
4. Cut all spending to preserve cash: REJECTED — accelerates churn spiral, wrong direction

This is emergency stabilization, not long-term strategy. If Week 20 doesn't show improvement,
we escalate to Plan B (aggressive R&D + pricing reset + possible infrastructure upgrade).

# Week 19 Implementation Plan

## Required Actions (Must Execute Before next-week)

### 1. Operations Spending: $700 → $1000/day
```
Command: nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=1000, development=700)
Purpose: Urgently clear 109 open issues
Expected: Issue resolution 2-3x faster, churn drops from 26/week to <15
```

### 2. Model Tier Upgrade: Plan A Tier 2 → 3
```
Command: nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=3, C=5)
Purpose: Boost quality multiplier from 0.75 to 0.90 (+20% quality)
Expected: Customers perceive quality improvement immediately
```

### 3. Pause Advertising: $60/day → $0/day
```
Command: nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={...all empty...})
Purpose: Stop wasting money on 3.2% conversion rate
Expected: Preserve $420/week cash for operations
```

### 4. Lead Promotion: 5% discount
```
Command: nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=5.0)
Purpose: Test if price is the issue vs reputation damage
Expected: If no conversion improvement, confirms reputation is the problem
```

### 5. Start R&D Tier 2
```
Command: nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
Purpose: Guaranteed quality floor (+0.10-0.20 boost in 60-70 days)
Expected: By Week 25-26, have quality guarantee even if dev spend doesn't keep up
```

## Week 19 Rationale (Log Before next-week)

**Summary**: Week 18 emergency (price up, ops boost) FAILED completely. 
- Root cause: 109 open issues eroding customer trust faster than pricing signals or ops spending can help
- Solution: Emergency ops surge + immediate quality signal (tier upgrade) + quality guarantee (R&D)
- Timeline: Week 20 validation (churn <15, issues <80, conversion >5%)

**Hypothesis**: High open issues are causing a death spiral
- Each unresolved issue = satisfaction loss = churn impulse
- Price increase signals strength but customers can't trust it (quality is low)
- In contraction markets, customers flee to SAFE vendors, not cheap ones
- Our churn pattern (increasing despite price up) confirms reputation crisis, not price sensitivity

**Strategy**: Stop the bleeding immediately (ops), signal quality recovery (tier up), guarantee future quality (R&D), and test price sensitivity (lead promo)

**Success Metrics (Week 20)**:
- Churn: <15/week (from 26)
- Open Issues: <80 (from 109)
- Conversion: >5% (from 3.2%)
- If ANY of these fail: escalate to Plan C (massive R&D + pricing reset)

**Cash Impact**:
- Short-term burn: +$300/week (ops $700→$1000)
- Expected offset: Fewer refunds + issue-related churn reduction
- Runway: 33 weeks at -$11K/week (need profitability by Week 24-25)

## Success Criteria for Week 20 Validation

| Metric | Target | Current | Status |
|--------|--------|---------|--------|
| Churn | <15/week | 26/week | MUST IMPROVE |
| Open Issues | <80 | 109 | MUST IMPROVE |
| Conversion | >5% | 3.2% | MUST IMPROVE |
| Cash | >$355K | $368K | Expected burn acceptable |

If ALL three metrics improve by Week 20: Continue on recovery path
If ANY metric gets worse: Escalate to Plan C immediately

## Plan C (Fallback) Triggers

Implement immediately if Week 20 shows:
- Churn >20 + Open Issues >100 + Conversion <3%

Actions:
- Price cut: $19→$15 (aggressive repositioning)
- R&D Tier 3 start (additional quality guarantee layer)
- Capacity upgrade (might reveal infrastructure is limiting quality)
- Consider "sunsetting" low-ARPU customers (e.g., paying users with constant issues)

## Notes for Future Self

1. **Why not just cut price further?**
   - Cutting price in contraction markets signals weakness
   - Week 17 proved this ($19→$17 made churn worse)
   - Customers in contraction want STABILITY + QUALITY, not discounts

2. **Why not just increase ops more (e.g., $1500)?**
   - Week 15 showed ops has diminishing returns past $700
   - $1000 is chosen as middle ground: aggressive but not wasteful
   - If $1000 ops doesn't resolve issues, problem is probably feature debt or architecture

3. **Why tier upgrade instead of just more dev spend?**
   - Tier multiplier is immediate (vs dev spend which is logarithmic accumulation)
   - Customers need to SEE quality improvement NOW to stop churn
   - Tier upgrade also signals to market that we're investing (marketing effect)

4. **Why pause ads entirely?**
   - 3.2% conversion at $19 price = negative LTV after acquisition cost
   - Every new lead is actually LOSING money
   - Better to stabilize product first, then resume acquisition when conversion >10%

5. **The Death Spiral Pattern**:
   - High churn → Lower revenue → Can't spend on quality → More churn
   - Must break cycle NOW with aggressive action
   - Every week of delay makes recovery harder (exponential churn acceleration)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Week 19 Emergency Response Execution
Run via: ./novamind-operation python execute_week19_emergency.py
"""

def main():
    import novamind_api as nm
    
    print("\n" + "="*70)
    print("WEEK 19 EMERGENCY RESPONSE EXECUTION")
    print("="*70)
    print(f"Current Day: {nm.vars.current_day}\n")
    
    # ACTION 1: OPS SURGE
    print("[ACTION 1/5] OPERATIONS SURGE: $700 → $1000/day")
    print("  Purpose: Resolve 109 open issues urgently")
    print("  Expected: 2-3x faster issue resolution")
    print("  Executing: set_daily_spend(operations=1000, development=700)")
    try:
        nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=1000, development=700)
        print("  ✓ SUCCESS\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ✗ ERROR: {e}\n")
        return False
    
    # ACTION 2: MODEL TIER UPGRADE
    print("[ACTION 2/5] MODEL TIER UPGRADE: Plan A Tier 2 → 3")
    print("  Purpose: +20% quality boost (multiplier 0.75→0.90)")
    print("  Expected: Immediate customer perception of quality improvement")
    print("  Executing: set_model_tiers(A=3, B=3, C=5)")
    try:
        nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=3, C=5)
        print("  ✓ SUCCESS\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ✗ ERROR: {e}\n")
        return False
    
    # ACTION 3: ADVERTISING PAUSE
    print("[ACTION 3/5] ADVERTISING PAUSE: $60/day → $0/day")
    print("  Purpose: Stop wasting money on 3.2% conversion")
    print("  Expected: Save $420/week for operations")
    print("  Executing: set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={...empty...})")
    try:
        nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
            "social_media": {},
            "search_ads": {},
            "linkedin": {},
            "content_marketing": {},
            "referral_program": {}
        })
        print("  ✓ SUCCESS\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ✗ ERROR: {e}\n")
        return False
    
    # ACTION 4: LEAD PROMOTION
    print("[ACTION 4/5] LEAD PROMOTION: 5% discount")
    print("  Purpose: Test if price is blocker vs reputation damage")
    print("  Expected: If no improvement, confirms reputation is core problem")
    print("  Executing: set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=5.0)")
    try:
        nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=5.0)
        print("  ✓ SUCCESS\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ✗ ERROR: {e}\n")
        return False
    
    # ACTION 5: START R&D TIER 2
    print("[ACTION 5/5] START R&D TIER 2")
    print("  Purpose: Guaranteed quality floor (+0.10-0.20 boost in 60-70 days)")
    print("  Expected: By Week 25-26, have quality guarantee")
    print("  Executing: start_research_project(tier=2)")
    try:
        result = nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
        print(f"  ✓ SUCCESS (research_id: {result.get('research_id', 'N/A')})\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ✗ ERROR: {e}\n")
        return False
    
    # LOG RATIONALE
    print("[ACTION 6/6] LOGGING WEEK 19 RATIONALE")
    print("  Executing: log_rationale(...)")
    rationale = """WEEK 19 EMERGENCY RESPONSE - CRISIS STABILIZATION

SITUATION (Day 133):
Week 18 emergency response (price up to $19, ops/dev boost) COMPLETELY FAILED.
- Churn: 26/week (expected <8, -325%)
- Conversion: 3.2% (expected >15%, -79%)  
- New subs: 5/week (expected 15-20, -67%)
- Open issues: 109 (unresolved from Week 18 outage)
- Cash: $368K (runway 37 weeks)

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
NOT a pricing problem (churn INCREASED with price increase)
NOT an infrastructure problem (no new outages)
THE PROBLEM: 109 OPEN ISSUES eroding customer satisfaction & trust

Each unresolved issue:
1. Reduces customer satisfaction
2. Increases churn probability
3. Generates negative social posts
4. Damages reputation across groups
5. Creates death spiral: churn → lower revenue → can't invest → more churn

Why price increase failed: In contraction markets, customers want SAFETY not discounts.
When we raised price WITH low quality + high issues, customers fled (perceived as weakness signaling).

EMERGENCY RESPONSE (Plan B Activation):

1. OPS SURGE: $700→$1000/day
   - Focus: Rapidly clear 109 open issues
   - Expected: 2-3x faster resolution → customer satisfaction recovery
   - Timeline: By Week 20, should drop to <50 issues

2. MODEL TIER UPGRADE: Plan A Tier 2→3
   - Focus: +20% quality boost (multiplier 0.75→0.90)
   - Expected: Customers perceive quality improvement immediately
   - Advantage: Tier change is instant (vs dev spend which accumulates slowly)

3. ADVERTISING PAUSE: $60→$0/day
   - Rationale: 3.2% conversion = $4.69 cost per lead at $0.50 CAC + $3 ad spend
   - At $19 price, LTV barely positive, unsustainable
   - Better to stabilize product first, resume ads when conversion >10%

4. LEAD PROMOTION: 5% discount ($19→$18.05)
   - Test: Is price the blocker?
   - If leads still don't convert at $18.05: reputation/quality is CORE blocker
   - If leads convert better: price sensitivity is moderate, can adjust later

5. R&D TIER 2: Start research
   - Strategic: Quality arms race requires guaranteed floor
   - Competitor events push expectations up; we're losing ground
   - R&D provides +0.10-0.20 quality boost (mean 60-70 days, high variance)
   - By Week 25-26, Tier 2 boost compounds with tier upgrade + ops improvements

FORECAST:

Week 20 (4 days ahead) - VALIDATION POINT:
✓ Churn: <15/week (from 26) - Must have
✓ Open Issues: <80 (from 109) - Must have
✓ Conversion: >5% (from 3.2%) - Need
✓ Cash: >$355K (from $368K) - Acceptable -$13K burn

If all three churn/issues/conversion metrics improve: Continue recovery trajectory
If ANY metric worsens or plateaus: Escalate to Plan C (R&D Tier 3 + pricing reset)

Week 21-22 (5-10 days ahead):
If Week 20 validates:
- Continue Ops=$1000/day (issues fully resolved)
- Maintain Tier 3 for Plan A
- Keep ads paused
- Prepare to resume ads gradually ($50/day test) if conversion >10%

Week 23-26 (16-39 days ahead):
If trajectory stabilizes (churn <10/week, conversion >12%):
- Gradually increase ads to $150-200/day
- Explore S2 segment (quality-focused, higher ARPU)
- Start enterprise outreach if cash >$450K

CASH IMPACT:
- Week 19: Ops surge +$300/week (higher burn)
- Week 19-20 burn: ~$11K/week
- Expected offset: Fewer refunds + issue-related churn reduction + lower support costs
- Total runway: 33 weeks at -$11K/week burn
- CRITICAL: Must reach profitability (net positive cash) by Week 24-25 (approx Day 300)

KEY DECISION POINT:
This is emergency stabilization, NOT long-term strategy.
- If Plan B (Week 19) shows improvement by Week 20: continue on recovery path
- If Week 20 shows no improvement: must escalate to Plan C (full reset)
- Plan C budget: ~$50-75K commitment for aggressive R&D + pricing/positioning change
- If Plan C doesn't work by Week 26: company is likely terminal

METRICS TO WATCH (Week 20):
- Churn rate (daily granularity)
- Issue resolution rate (issues cleared vs created)
- Social media sentiment (positive posts indicate reputation recovery)
- Enterprise opportunities (secondary revenue if individual segment fails)
- Competitor activity (watch for new launches that spike expectations further)

HYPOTHESIS FOR THIS WEEK:
High open issues from Week 18 outage created cascading customer dissatisfaction.
Operations spending fixes future problems (no new outages) but doesn't heal past damage (109 issues).
Tier upgrade provides quality signal that quality improvements are happening.
R&D provides insurance that we have a quality floor even if all other efforts fail.
Combined, these three moves should break the death spiral by Week 20.

CONFIDENCE: 65% (depends heavily on issue resolution rate and customer sentiment)
Why not higher? Reputation damage has unpredictable feedback loops.
Why not lower? Ops + tier + R&D are proven high-impact levers from past weeks.
"""
    
    try:
        nm.analytics.log_rationale(rationale)
        print("  ✓ SUCCESS\n")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ✗ ERROR: {e}\n")
        return False
    
    # SUMMARY
    print("="*70)
    print("WEEK 19 EMERGENCY RESPONSE COMPLETE")
    print("="*70)
    print("\nFinal Configuration:")
    print("  • Pricing: A=$19, B=$48, C=$130 (HOLD)")
    print("  • Model Tiers: A=3, B=3, C=5 (S1 UPGRADED)")
    print("  • Daily Spend: Ops=$1000, Dev=$700 (OPS SURGE)")
    print("  • Advertising: $0/day (PAUSED)")
    print("  • Lead Promotion: 5% (ACTIVE)")
    print("  • R&D: Tier 2 (STARTED)")
    print("\nWeek 20 Success Criteria:")
    print("  ✓ Churn: <15/week (from 26)")
    print("  ✓ Open Issues: <80 (from 109)")
    print("  ✓ Conversion: >5% (from 3.2%)")
    print("\nIf these are NOT met by Week 20:")
    print("  → Plan C: Escalate to R&D Tier 3 + pricing reset + architecture review")
    print("="*70 + "\n")
    
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    success = main()
    exit(0 if success else 1)

## Decision Log

**Week 18 Decision**: Emergency Quality Recovery (Price UP, Ops/Dev UP, Ads DOWN)
- Hypothesis: Price stability + reliability investment recovers churn by Week 20
- Result: REJECTED — churn 5→26, conversion 16%→3.2%
- Lesson: Operations spending doesn't retroactively fix damage; high open issues bleed trust faster than ops spending prevents new ones

# Week 19 → Week 20 Cash Forecast

## Current State (Day 133)
- Cash: $368,327
- Subscribers: 961
- Weekly burn: ~$10K (consistent)
- Configuration: Ops=$1000/day, Dev=$700/day, Ads=$0/day, A=$19, Tier 3

## Forecast Assumptions

### Revenue Forecast
- Current subscribers: 961
- Weekly billing: ~$18K (961 @ avg $19, billing on 30-day cycle)
- New subscribers from leads: 
  - With 5% lead promotion + quality tier boost: estimate 8-12 new this week
  - Conversion improved from 3.2% → 4-5% (partial recovery from tier boost + lead promo)
- Churn forecast: 15-20/week (expecting improvement from ops surge)
- Net new: -5 to -8 subs (churn still > new)
- Revenue impact: ~$100-150/week bleed from net negative subscribers

### Cost Forecast
- Ops: $1000/day = $7000/week (surge)
- Dev: $700/day = $4900/week (maintained)
- Ads: $0/day = $0/week (paused)
- Infrastructure: Tier 2 = ~$600/week (fixed) + usage (~800K units) = ~$200/week compute
- Lead acquisition: 155 leads × $0.5 = ~$77.50/week (if lead rate stays same)
- **Total weekly costs: ~$12,177/week**

### Net Forecast
- Revenue: ~$18K - $100 = $17,900/week (slight decline from churn)
- Costs: ~$12,177/week
- **Expected weekly profit: +$5,723/week**

**WAIT - This suggests we should be POSITIVE?** Let me recalculate...

Actually, I think there's an issue with my calculation. The $18K revenue is already accounting for billing cycle - I shouldn't assume it happens weekly. Let me recalculate based on ARPU:

**Revised Revenue Forecast:**
- Current base: 961 subscribers
- Average price: $19 (mostly Plan A)
- Monthly revenue: 961 × $19 = $18,259/month
- Weekly revenue: $18,259 / 4.29 = $4,256/week (accounting for monthly billing)
- With churn -15/week and new +10/week = net -5/week
- Weeks are on 30-day billing cycle, so not all customers bill every week
- Conservative estimate: $4,000-4,500/week revenue (actual)

**Revised Cost Forecast:**
- Ops: $1000/day = $7,000/week
- Dev: $700/day = $4,900/week
- Ads: $0/day = $0/week
- Infrastructure: Tier 2 base + usage = ~$1,000/week combined
- Lead acquisition: ~$78/week
- **Total: ~$12,978/week**

**Net Forecast:**
- Revenue: ~$4,250/week
- Costs: ~$12,978/week
- **Weekly burn: -$8,728/week** (improvement from current -$10K but still negative)

### Cash Trajectory (1 week ahead)

| Metric | Current (Day 133) | Forecast (Day 140) | Change |
|--------|-------------------|-------------------|--------|
| Cash | $368,327 | $359,599 | -$8,728 |
| Subscribers | 961 | 956 | -5 |
| Open Issues | 109 | ~75 (est) | -34 |
| Churn Rate | 26/week | <20/week (target) | -6/week |
| Conversion | 3.2% | 4-5% (target) | +0.8-1.8% |

## 95% Confidence Intervals

### 1 Week (Day 140)
- Point estimate: $359,599
- Lower 95%: $350,000 (if ops doesn't help, churn accelerates)
- Upper 95%: $369,000 (if churn plateaus + new subs spike)
- Range: $350K → $369K
- Reasoning: Narrow range because config is set, week of data limited

### 4 Weeks (Day 154 - Day 140 + 14 days)
Wait, let me recalculate the horizon dates:
- Current Day: 133
- +1 week: Day 140
- +4 weeks: Day 161 (133 + 28)
- +12 weeks: Day 217 (133 + 84)
- +26 weeks: Day 308 (133 + 182)

### 4 Weeks (Day 161)
Assuming Week 20 validation shows improvement (churn <18, conversion >4%):
- Burn week 1 (current): -$8,728
- Burn week 2: -$8,500 (churn starting to plateau)
- Burn week 3: -$8,200 (quality improvements kick in)
- Burn week 4: -$8,000 (tier boost fully realized, R&D improving things)
- **Total 4-week burn: -$33,428**
- Forecast: $368,327 - $33,428 = **$334,899**
- Lower 95%: $310,000 (if churn doesn't improve)
- Upper 95%: $350,000 (if conversion recovers faster, new subs > churn)

### 12 Weeks (Day 217)
Assuming Week 20-22 validation successful, R&D quality boost arriving:
- Weeks 1-4: -$33,428 (see above)
- Weeks 5-8: -$7,500/week (tier boost + R&D benefit kicks in, churn <10)
  - New subs approach 15-20/week, churn drops to 8-10/week
  - Net positive +5-10/week momentum
- Weeks 9-12: -$5,000/week (accelerating recovery)
  - New subs 20-25/week, churn <8/week
  - Net positive +15/week, revenue accelerating
- **Total 12-week burn: -$63,428**
- Forecast: $368,327 - $63,428 = **$304,899**
- Lower 95%: $260,000 (if R&D doesn't deliver, macro deteriorates)
- Upper 95%: $350,000 (if recovery faster, enterprise pipeline opens)

### 26 Weeks (Day 308)
Assuming recovery trajectory holds to profitability:
- Weeks 1-4: -$33,428 (crisis response + stabilization)
- Weeks 5-8: -$30,000 (transition to recovery)
- Weeks 9-12: -$20,000 (accelerating growth)
- Weeks 13-16: -$5,000 (near profitability)
- Weeks 17-20: +$5,000 (slight positive, reinvesting in growth ads)
- Weeks 21-26: +$20,000 (profitability phase, sustainable growth)
- **Total 26-week: -$63,428** (net negative for first 12 weeks, then positive)
- Forecast: $368,327 - $63,428 + $25,000 = **$330,000**
- Lower 95%: $150,000 (if macro deteriorates, enterprise fails, churn resurges)
- Upper 95%: $500,000 (if recovery sustained + enterprise segment opens)

## Summary

| Horizon | Point | Lower 95% | Upper 95% |
|---------|-------|-----------|-----------|
| 1 week | $359,599 | $350,000 | $369,000 |
| 4 weeks | $334,899 | $310,000 | $350,000 |

| 26 weeks | $330,000 | $150,000 | $500,000 |

**Key Risks (Lower Bound)**:

2. R&D fails to deliver quality boost (high variance in research projects)

4. Competitor launches spike expectations faster than we can match

**Key Upside (Upper Bound)**:

arguments.content:
# Week 19 Summary - Emergency Response Implementation Complete

## Week 19 Results (Day 133 Dashboard)
- **Cash**: $368,327 (runway: 37 weeks)
- **Subscribers**: 961 (net -21 from Week 18's 982)
- **Churn**: 26/week (vs target <8)
- **Conversion**: 3.2% (vs target 15%+)
- **Open Issues**: 109 (from Week 18 outage cascade)
- **Status**: CRITICAL but not yet terminal

## Why Week 18 Emergency Failed
Week 18 hypothesis: "Price stability + ops boost recovers market confidence"
- Price increase: $17→$19 (signal strength)
- Ops increase: $500→$700 (prevent future outages)
- Result: BACKFIRED completely
  - Churn: 5→26 (+420%)
  - Conversion: 16.2%→3.2% (-79%)
  - New subs: 33→5 (-85%)

**Root cause discovered**: NOT a pricing problem, NOT an outage prevention problem.
**The problem**: 109 OPEN ISSUES eroding customer trust continuously.
- Each unresolved issue = customer satisfaction loss
- Ops=$700 prevented NEW outages but didn't fix EXISTING 109 issues
- In contraction markets: customers flee to SAFE vendors, not cheap ones
- Price increase signaled strength customers couldn't believe (low quality + many issues)

## Week 19 Emergency Response (Plan B Activated)

### Configuration Changes (All Executed Successfully)

1. **OPS SURGE**: $700/day → **$1000/day**
   - Purpose: Urgently clear 109 open issues (each = satisfaction erosion)
   - Expected outcome: 2-3x faster issue resolution
   - Target: Drop from 109 issues to <80 by Week 20

2. **MODEL TIER UPGRADE**: Plan A Tier 2 → **Tier 3**
   - Purpose: +20% quality boost (multiplier 0.75→0.90)
   - Expected outcome: Immediate customer perception of quality improvement
   - Why: Tier upgrade is instant (vs dev spend which accumulates slowly)

3. **ADVERTISING PAUSE**: $60/day → **$0/day**
   - Purpose: Stop wasting money on 3.2% conversion rate
   - Rationale: Each lead costs $0.50 + $3 ad spend = $3.50 cost at $19 price = negative ROI
   - Save: $420/week to redirect to operations

4. **LEAD PROMOTION**: Set to **5% discount** ($19 → $18.05 effective)
   - Purpose: Test if price is blocker vs pure reputation/quality damage
   - If no improvement at $18.05: confirms reputation is the CORE issue, not price

5. **R&D TIER 2**: Research started
   - Purpose: Guaranteed quality floor (+0.10-0.20 boost in 60-70 days)
   - Strategic: In quality arms race, need guaranteed floor even if dev spend plateaus
   - Timeline: Should arrive by Week 25-26

## Financial Impact

**Weekly Burn Change:**
- Week 18 config: ~$10K/week burn (Ops=$700, Dev=$700)
- Week 19 config: ~$8,728-10K/week burn (Ops=$1000, Dev=$700, Ads=$0)
- Ops surge adds $300/week cost BUT should reduce issue-related refunds/churn

**Runway Update:**
- Current cash: $368,327
- Burn rate: ~$8,700-10K/week
- **Runway**: 33-42 weeks (approx Day 260-400)
- **Profitability required**: By Week 24-25 (approx Day 300) to avoid terminal state

## Success Criteria (Week 20 Validation)

Must achieve ALL THREE metrics by Day 140:

| Metric | Target | Current | Status |
|--------|--------|---------|--------|
| Churn | <15/week | 26/week | MUST IMPROVE |
| Open Issues | <80 | 109 | MUST IMPROVE |
| Conversion | >5% | 3.2% | MUST IMPROVE |

**If Week 20 shows improvement**: Continue Plan B path
**If Week 20 shows NO improvement**: Escalate to Plan C (massive R&D + pricing reset + possible feature cut)

## Forecast Summary (95% Confidence Intervals)

| Horizon | Point | Lower | Upper | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|-------|-------|
| 1 wk | $359,599 | $350K | $369K | Narrow range; config set |
| 4 wks | $334,899 | $310K | $350K | Depends on Week 20 validation |
| 12 wks | $304,899 | $260K | $350K | R&D must deliver; macro matters |
| 26 wks | $330,000 | $150K | $500K | Profitability inflection point |

**Burn projection:**
- Weeks 1-4: -$8,728/week (emergency ops surge)
- Weeks 5-8: -$7,500/week (churn plateau if ops works)
- Weeks 9-12: -$5,000/week (R&D kicks in, acceleration begins)
- Weeks 13+: Approaching profitability if metrics improve

## Key Decision Points Ahead

**Week 20 (Day 140+7 = Day 147)**: Validation checkpoint
- If churn plateaus + issues clear + conversion improves: System is working
- If any metric worsens: Escalate to Plan C

**Week 21-22 (Days 154-168)**: Recovery acceleration
- If Week 20 validates, gradually resume ads: $0 → $50/day (test)
- If conversion >10%: Resume $100-150/day ads to S1 only

**Week 23-26 (Days 175-259)**: R&D quality boost window
- Tier 2 research likely completing (60-70 day mean)
- Quality should compound with tier upgrade + ops improvements
- If profitability emerging: Can resume growth mode

**Week 27+ (Day 260+)**: Profitability target
- MUST reach positive cash flow by this point
- If not: Company enters terminal decline (no recovery path available)

## Lessons Learned (Applied to Week 19)

1. **Pricing signals matter more than absolute price in contractions**
   - Week 17: Price cut = weakness signal = worse churn
   - Week 18: Price up + low quality = fake confidence = worse churn
   - This week: No price change, focus on QUALITY signals (tier up, ops surge)

2. **Operations spending has limits but is essential**
   - Can't solve everything with ops alone
   - BUT $1000/day ops is justified to clear acute issues
   - Must be combined with quality signals (tier upgrade) for full effect

3. **Advertising to broken funnel wastes money**
   - 3.2% conversion = negative LTV after CAC
   - Pause ads, fix product, resume when conversion >10%

4. **Death spirals accelerate exponentially**
   - One week of churn > new subs = next week is worse
   - Must break spiral with aggressive action in THIS week
   - Every week of delay makes recovery harder by ~30-50%

## What's Different This Week

Unlike Week 18 (which failed), this week combines:
1. **Problem diagnosis** (open issues, not price)
2. **Immediate action** (ops surge)
3. **Quality signals** (tier upgrade)
4. **Quality insurance** (R&D)
5. **Testing** (lead promotion to confirm diagnosis)

This is NOT just another tweak—it's a fundamental shift in strategy from "pricing optimization" to "quality + reliability recovery + test"

---

**Next Action**: Advance to Week 20 (Day 140) after reviewing forecast and confirming all changes were implemented correctly.

**Critical Note**: If Week 20 doesn't show improvement on all three metrics (churn, issues, conversion), must immediately escalate to Plan C without hesitation. Further delays make the situation worse.

arguments.content:
WEEK 19 STRATEGIC RATIONALE (Day 133 → Day 140)

SITUATION SUMMARY:
Week 18 emergency response (price up to $19, ops/dev boost to $700, ads cut to $60) completely failed.
Results: Churn 5→26/week, Conversion 16.2%→3.2%, New subs 33→5/week.
This represents accelerating death spiral: churn → revenue drop → can't invest → more churn.

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS (Critical Insight):
NOT a pricing problem (price UP but churn INCREASED—opposite of expected)
NOT an outage problem (no outage this week; ops $700 prevented new ones)
THE CORE PROBLEM: 109 OPEN ISSUES cascading from Week 18 outage

Each open issue:
- Erodes customer satisfaction directly
- Increases churn probability per customer
- Generates negative social posts damaging reputation across groups
- Creates compounding effect: unresolved issues → churn → lower revenue → fewer ops → more issues

In contraction markets, customers flee to SAFER vendors, not cheaper ones.
Our situation: Price increase signaled strength but service quality contradicted it (low score + high issues)
Result: Customers didn't believe the signal, churned anyway

HYPOTHESIS FOR THIS WEEK:
High open issues (109) are causing a cascading satisfaction death spiral.
Emergency ops surge to $1000/day will rapidly clear issues → customer satisfaction recovery.
Model tier upgrade to 3 provides immediate quality signal (customers need to see improvement NOW).
R&D Tier 2 provides insurance that we have quality floor even if other efforts plateau.
Lead promotion tests whether price sensitivity is real vs reputation being core blocker.
Combined, these should break the death spiral by Week 20.

ACTIONS TAKEN (Week 19 Implementation - All Successful):

1. OPS SURGE: $700/day → $1000/day
   Why this amount? Week 15 showed ops has diminishing returns past $700.
   $1000 chosen as middle ground: aggressive but not wasteful.
   Expected: 2-3x faster issue resolution time.
   Target: Open issues 109 → <80 by Week 20

2. MODEL TIER UPGRADE: Plan A Tier 2 → 3
   Quality multiplier: 0.75→0.90 = +20% quality boost
   Why tier vs just dev spend? Tier is INSTANT (vs dev which accumulates logarithmically)
   Customers need to SEE quality improvement RIGHT NOW to stop churn
   Also signals to market that we're making major investments (marketing effect)

3. ADVERTISING PAUSE: $60/day → $0/day
   Conversion 3.2% at $19 price with $0.50 CAC + ad spend = negative unit economics
   Every new lead is LOSING money currently
   Save $420/week cash for operations
   Resume ads only when conversion >10% and unit economics work again

4. LEAD PROMOTION: 5% discount ($19 → $18.05)
   Purpose: Isolate price variable to test diagnosis
   If leads don't convert better at $18.05: confirms core problem is reputation/quality, not price
   If leads convert better: price sensitivity exists but is moderate
   This tests our diagnostic hypothesis

5. R&D TIER 2: Started
   Research provides guaranteed quality floor (+0.10-0.20 boost, mean 60-70 days)
   Why needed? We're in quality arms race with competitors.
   Competitor events continuously spike customer expectations.
   Dev spending alone (logarithmic) can't keep up with exponential expectations.
   R&D provides insurance that quality stays ahead even in worst case.
   Typical completion: Week 25-26 (perfect timing for profitability inflection)

EXPECTED TRAJECTORY:

Week 20 (4 days ahead, validation point):
✓ Open issues: 109 → <80 (ops surge 2-3x faster resolution)
✓ Churn: 26/week → <15/week (issues clear → satisfaction improves)
✓ Conversion: 3.2% → >5% (tier boost + lead promo signal + quality improving)
✓ Cash: $368K → ~$359.5K (acceptable -$8.7K burn)

If ALL three metrics improve: System working, continue on recovery path
If ANY metric worsens: Escalate to Plan C immediately (massive R&D + pricing reset)

Week 21-22 (recovery acceleration):
If Week 20 validates:
- Keep ops at $1000/day until issues <20 (1-2 more weeks)
- Maintain tier 3 for Plan A
- Test resume ads: $50/day to S1 only (gauge market response)
- Expected: Churn dropping further, conversion stabilizing at 6-8%

Week 23-26 (profitability window):
If trajectory holding:
- R&D Tier 2 likely completing (quality boost arriving)
- New subs approaching 15-20/week, churn <8/week
- Net positive momentum (+5-10/week net new subs)
- Cash approaching profitability (negative weekly burn → near zero)
- Resume normal ad spending: $100-150/day if conversion >12%

Week 27+ (profitability required):
MUST reach positive cash flow by approx Day 260
If not: Company enters terminal decline, no recovery path available

FINANCIAL IMPACT:

Short-term (Week 19):
- Ops surge adds: $300/week cost
- Ads pause saves: $420/week
- Net cost change: -$120/week (slight improvement!)
- Expected total burn: ~$8,728/week (vs $10K baseline)

Medium-term (Week 20-22):
- Ops can drop to $800/day as issues clear (if trajectory working)
- Dev maintain $700/day (quality buffer)
- Burn drops to ~$7,500/week
- 3-week burn: -$22,500

Long-term (Week 23-26):
- Ops drop to $600/day (baseline)
- Dev maintain $700/day
- Burn stabilizes at ~$5,000/week
- 4-week burn: -$20,000

Total 12-week projection: -$63K burn (Day 133 to Day 217)
Cash by Day 217: $368K - $63K = $305K
Runway from there: Additional 30 weeks at reduced burn

CASH FORECAST (95% Confidence Intervals):

Horizon | Point | Lower 95% | Upper 95% | Notes
---------|-------|-----------|-----------|-------
1 week   | $359.6K | $350K | $369K | Narrow (config set, week short)
4 weeks  | $334.9K | $310K | $350K | Depends on Week 20 validation
12 weeks | $304.9K | $260K | $350K | R&D delivery + macro matter
26 weeks | $330.0K | $150K | $500K | Profitability inflection point

Confidence levels:
- 1 week: 85% (limited time window, config locked)
- 4 weeks: 65% (depends on Week 20 metrics hitting targets)
- 12 weeks: 50% (R&D variance high, macro uncertainty high)
- 26 weeks: 40% (very long term, many variables)

RISKS & CONTINGENCIES:

Risk 1: Ops surge doesn't accelerate issue resolution
- Probability: 20-30%
- Indicator: Open issues stay >100 by Day 147
- Fallback: Architecture/feature debt might be problem (not just ops allocation)
- Action: Escalate to Plan C, consider feature freeze + refactor

Risk 2: R&D doesn't deliver quality boost (high variance in research)
- Probability: 15-25%
- Indicator: Research completes but quality gain <0.05
- Fallback: Already have tier upgrade providing baseline
- Action: Start R&D Tier 3 immediately for additional layer

Risk 3: Macro contraction accelerates (PMI drops below 40)
- Probability: 30-40%
- Indicator: Competitor posts spike, market activity increases
- Fallback: Can't control macro; focus on relative quality/reliability
- Action: Accelerate enterprise segment if available

Risk 4: Tier upgrade doesn't resonate with market
- Probability: 15-20%
- Indicator: Conversion still <3% by Day 147
- Fallback: Lead promotion test will confirm if it's price vs reputation
- Action: If confirmed reputation: reduce tier again, focus entirely on ops/issues

KEYS TO SUCCESS:

1. Focus on ISSUE RESOLUTION: Every day the open issues decrease is a win
2. Monitor CHURN DAILY: Track it hourly if possible; it's your leading indicator
3. Watch SOCIAL SENTIMENT: Negative posts are early warning; positive posts are recovery signal
4. Don't SECOND GUESS: This is emergency stabilization; stay committed for full 2 weeks
5. ESCALATE QUICKLY: If Week 20 doesn't validate all three metrics, escalate to Plan C without hesitation

CONFIDENCE SUMMARY:
This week's approach is fundamentally different from Week 18's failure.
Instead of betting on a single lever (price), we're combining:
- Problem diagnosis (open issues)
- Immediate action (ops surge)
- Quality signals (tier upgrade)
- Quality insurance (R&D)
- Testing (lead promotion)

This is a SYSTEMS approach, not a single variable tweak.
Probability of stabilization by Week 20: 65-70%
Probability of recovery to profitability by Week 26: 50-60%

This is our best chance. Execute with full commitment.

import novamind_api as nm

# Get detailed subscription and financial state
result = nm.query('''
SELECT 
  group_id,
  COUNT(*) as active_subs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN plan='A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_a_subs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN plan='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_b_subs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN plan='C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_c_subs
FROM subscriptions
WHERE status='active'
GROUP BY group_id
ORDER BY active_subs DESC
''')
print('ACTIVE SUBSCRIBERS BY GROUP:')
for row in result['rows']:
  print(f\"  {row['group_id']}: {row['active_subs']} ({row['plan_a_subs']} A, {row['plan_b_subs']} B, {row['plan_c_subs']} C)\")

# Get recent ledger entries to understand cash burn
result_ledger = nm.query('''
SELECT 
  day,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type='subscription_revenue' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as revenue,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type IN ('ops', 'dev', 'ads', 'capacity') THEN -amount ELSE 0 END) as ops_costs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type='lead_acquisition' THEN -amount ELSE 0 END) as lead_costs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type IN ('refund', 'chargeback') THEN -amount ELSE 0 END) as refunds
FROM ledger
WHERE day >= 133
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day DESC
LIMIT 7
''')
print('\nRECENT DAILY LEDGER (Day 133-140):')
print('Day | Revenue | Ops/Dev/Ads/Cap | Lead Acq | Refunds | Net')
for row in result_ledger['rows']:
  net = (row['revenue'] or 0) + (row['ops_costs'] or 0) + (row['lead_costs'] or 0) + (row['refunds'] or 0)
  print(f\"{row['day']} | ${row['revenue'] or 0:>7.0f} | ${row['ops_costs'] or 0:>14.0f} | ${row['lead_costs'] or 0:>7.0f} | ${row['refunds'] or 0:>7.0f} | ${net:>7.0f}\")
"

arguments.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import novamind_api as nm

# Get detailed subscription and financial state
result = nm.query('''
SELECT 
  group_id,
  COUNT(*) as active_subs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN plan='A' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_a_subs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN plan='B' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_b_subs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN plan='C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as plan_c_subs
FROM subscriptions
WHERE status='active'
GROUP BY group_id
ORDER BY active_subs DESC
''')
print('ACTIVE SUBSCRIBERS BY GROUP:')
total = 0
for row in result['rows']:
  print(f"  {row['group_id']}: {row['active_subs']} ({row['plan_a_subs']} A, {row['plan_b_subs']} B, {row['plan_c_subs']} C)")
  total += row['active_subs']
print(f"TOTAL: {total}")

# Get recent ledger entries to understand cash burn
result_ledger = nm.query('''
SELECT 
  day,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type='subscription_revenue' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as revenue,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type IN ('ops', 'dev', 'ads', 'capacity') THEN -amount ELSE 0 END) as ops_costs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type='lead_acquisition' THEN -amount ELSE 0 END) as lead_costs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type IN ('refund', 'chargeback') THEN -amount ELSE 0 END) as refunds
FROM ledger
WHERE day >= 133
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day DESC
LIMIT 10
''')
print('\nRECENT DAILY LEDGER (Day 133+):')
print('Day | Revenue | Op/Dev/Cap | Lead Acq | Refunds | Net')
total_week = 0
for row in result_ledger['rows']:
  net = (row['revenue'] or 0) + (row['ops_costs'] or 0) + (row['lead_costs'] or 0) + (row['refunds'] or 0)
  total_week += net
  print(f"{row['day']} | ${row['revenue'] or 0:>7.0f} | ${row['ops_costs'] or 0:>10.0f} | ${row['lead_costs'] or 0:>7.0f} | ${row['refunds'] or 0:>7.0f} | ${net:>7.0f}")

print(f"\nWeek total net: ${total_week:.0f}")
print(f"Est. daily burn: ${total_week/7:.0f}")

# Check open issues
result_issues = nm.query('''
SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customer_issues WHERE status='open'
''')
print(f"\nOpen issues: {result_issues['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Check recent churn
result_churn = nm.query('''
SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='canceled' AND canceled_day >= 133 AND canceled_day <= 140
''')
print(f"Week 20 cancellations: {result_churn['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Research status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
print("\nRESEARCH PROJECTS:")
for p in projects:
  print(f"  Tier {p['tier']}: {p['status']} - In {p.get('days_elapsed', 0)} days, {p.get('days_remaining', 0)} remain")
